pass1_value.note: Documentation explicitly lists OAuth 2.0 for third-party apps and Personal Access Tokens (used as bearer tokens). The API root also shows Authorization: Bearer <token> header requirement.

final_value.note: Harvest’s official developer documentation states the API supports both OAuth2 (for third-party applications) and personal access tokens (used via `Authorization: Bearer {token}` along with the required `Harvest-Account-ID` header).

pass1_value.note: Personal Access Tokens can be created via the user UI (Avatar → Personal Settings → API Credentials) which indicates a user can self-serve obtain credentials for their account. Pricing page does not state API access is restricted to paid plans.

final_value.note: Official docs state that a user can create a personal access token under their Profile (linking to https://id.getharvest.com/profile), indicating self-serve credential creation via the UI.

pass1_value.note: Public REST API documentation exists and Personal Access Tokens can be self-generated, enabling immediate development and testing.

final_value.note: Public Harvest API v2 documentation exists and the official authentication documentation confirms immediate usability via OAuth2 or self-created personal access tokens.
